mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate drops below 5.0 percent, labor market tightness accelerates wage growth and consumer spending, boosting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns within a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of instances where UNRATE crosses below 5.0 percent over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production combined with increasing retail sales indicates synchronized supply-demand expansion, which validates earnings growth forecasts and lifts equity valuations over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces a negative directional accuracy below 55 percent over any 3-year rolling window, indicating the interaction does not predict returns.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of active monetary tightening, rising short-term rates increase borrowing costs and compress equity multiples, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero during consecutive rate hike periods, invalidating the discount rate channel.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth signals expanding aggregate demand, which improves corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP revisions consistently show positive growth while SPX 5-day forward returns are negative in more than 50 percent of observations.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index rises by more than 1.0 percentage points over three months, manufacturing capacity utilization expands, reducing input cost inflation and supporting positive equity returns over a 20-day window.
failure_condition: The signal triggers positive SPX returns in fewer than 45 percent of cases during manufacturing upcycles, indicating a breakdown in the capacity utilization channel.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts reflects broad-based economic momentum, which sustains consumer credit expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined PAYEMS and HOUST growth signal yields a negative information coefficient over any 5-year rolling window, proving the interaction lacks predictive power.

mechanism_narrative: In high-inflation environments, rising consumer price indices erode real disposable income and trigger Federal Reserve hawkishness, which compresses equity valuations and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day window.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day forward returns remain positive or statistically neutral when CPIAUCSL exceeds a 3-year moving average threshold, refuting the inflation-valuation channel.
